seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lower Daintree and Lower Tenthill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lower Daintree vs Lower Tenthill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lower Daintree and Lower Tenthill.
heading: Lower Daintree vs Lower Tenthill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lower Daintree, Queensland and Lower Tenthill, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lower Daintree has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Lower Tenthill (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lower Daintree or Lower Tenthill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lower Daintree has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Lower Tenthill scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lower Daintree scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Lower Tenthill scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lower Daintree or Lower Tenthill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lower Daintree has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
